figures.caption: Fig. 3: FSD-MP planning results in Dense2D with extra obstacles. The model is trained only at resolution N = 64 and evaluated at multiple resolutions without retraining.
TABLE I: Benchmarking in different resolutions and environments
Fig. 4: Planning results of FSD-MP in 3D Franka arm environments. Orange lines are end-effector trajectories.
TABLE II: Ablation on Architecture and Diffusion Noise: Valid Rate (%) Across Resolution N

Fig. 2: Environments used in the motion planning experiments. Gray obstacles are present during training, while red obstacles are introduced only at inference time to evaluate generalization to unseen obstacles.

Fig. 1: Overview of FSD-MP. (Top) The forward process performs zero-end projection, transforms trajectories into DST coefficients and applies mode-wise diffusion with variance scaled by eigenvalue λk. (Bottom) The reverse process denoises via FiLM-conditioned DST-FNO layers, together with guidance based on the predicted clean trajectory x̂0. The function-space formulation enables discretization-invariant trajectory generation across both coarse and fine discretizations.
